name: Dylan Thomas
abstract: Dylan Marlais Thomas (27 October 1914 – 9 November 1953) was a Welsh poet and writer, whose works include the poems "Do not go gentle into that good night" and "And death shall have no dominion", as well as the "play for voices" Under Milk Wood. He also wrote stories and radio broadcasts such as A Child's Christmas in Wales and Portrait …

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: You could go to the Whitehorse Tavern, where Dylan Thomas – speaking of people drinking themselves to death – used to preside.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
